PackageFileKind.description: Package file classification used by manifests and later analyzers.

oneOf.description: Windows executable image or loadable library.
Symbolic link whose target semantics are part of package identity.

PackageFileRecord.description: Canonical evidence for one included package file or symbolic link.

executable.description: Whether the file is expected to contain directly loadable machine code.

normalized_path.description: Root-relative path using `/`, with original case preserved.

sha256.description: Content digest, or domain-separated link-target digest.

signer_thumbprint.description: Authenticode signer thumbprint when a later evidence stage supplies it.

size.description: File byte length, or canonical link-target text length for a symbolic link.

Sha256Digest.description: A SHA-256 digest serialized as lowercase `sha256:<hex>` text.

description:
Canonical package-tree identity assembled from pre-observed package records.

The manifest binds its ordered records and Merkle root, but does not by itself
prove that acquisition used an immutable filesystem snapshot. That provenance
remains a separate property of the producer.

format_version.description: Version of path, leaf, and directory canonicalization rules.

title: PackageTreeManifest
